name: the-china-study
description: T. Colin Campbell and Thomas M. Campbell II's 'The China Study: The Most Comprehensive Study of Nutrition Ever Conducted and Startling Implications for Diet, Weight Loss, and Long-Term Health' — the landmark book advocating a whole-food, plant-based diet. Based on the China Project, a massive epidemiological study linking animal protein to cancer, heart disease, diabetes, and other 'diseases of affluence.'
useCase: Understanding the link between diet and chronic disease, learning the benefits of a whole-food plant-based diet, critiquing the Western medical establishment's focus on treatment over prevention, and improving personal health through nutrition.
knownRisks: Some claims have been debated by other researchers
Very strict dietary recommendations may be hard to follow
The book's tone is polemical — positions as 'truth vs. establishment'
Not everyone agrees with the complete elimination of animal products
